wylie: re lcags pa or re'u lcags pa
english: Med., a mezereon with white blossoms in the South-Himalaya, of which paper is made.
prop. n., Triloknath, a much frequented place of pilgrimage in Chamba, with a famous image and sanctuary of Avalokiteśvara.

Cs. sbst., also ré-bo (re bo), acc. to some sbré-bo (sbre bo), W. *re-snam* (re snam), Cs. sack-cloth, a kind of cloth of yak's-hair, a tent-cloth (also re-ldé (re lde) and re-yól (re yol) Cs.); re-gúr (re gur) a tent of such cloth.

I. vb., 1. to hope, tams-cád mtóṅ-du reo (thams cad mthong du re'o) all hoped to see Dzl.; dé-la pán-du ré-nas (de la phan du re nas) hoping it might be good for it Mil.; sú-la re (su la re) in whom should they place their hope, in whom should they trust? loṅ yód-du ré-la (long yod du re la) whilst you are hoping still to have time (enough) Mil.; ré-żiṅ ltós-pa (re zhing ltos pa) to look up full of hope Glr. - 2. to wish, v. II. - 3. to beg, to ask alms, to go a begging, for victuals, *ḳo ré-a-la yoṅ* (kho re a la yong) W. he comes to beg. II. sbst. hope; wish, frq., ré-ba skóṅ-ba (re ba skong ba), ré-ba sgrúb-pa (re ba sgrub pa) to fulfil a hope; rnyéd-pa (rnyed pa), tób-pa (thob pa) to get it fulfilled, to obtain what one has hoped for, ré-ba ltar gyur (re ba ltar gyur) it goes to one's wish, as well as one could wish; ré-ba daṅ ldán-pa (re ba dang ldan pa) hoping, full of hope, ré-ba méd-pa (re ba med pa) hopeless, despairing. Comp. re-tág (re thag) v. tág-pa (thag pa). -- re-dógs (re dogs) hope and fear, re-dógs med (re dogs med) being without hope and without fear (the principal aim and prerogative of ascetics) Mil. -- ré-(bai) gnas (re ba'i gnas) Cs.: room for hope; prob. also = ré-sa (re sa) the person or thing whereon one's hopes are placed C., W.

turn, series, or more accurately: the order or change of the series, ṅéd-la sdúg-pai ré-mos bab (nged la sdug pa'i re mos bab) then misfortune came to be our turn Mil.; re-mos-su (re mos su) Pth., *ré-mos ċós-la* (re mos chos la) Ld. by turns, alternately, e.g. to strike one's breast with the hands; *ré-mos ré-mos* (re mos re mos) W. by degrees, gradually; re-móṅs (re mongs) id. Ma.
W. the spread- or warp-beam of a loom.
1. Sch.: reg-yzig-pa (reg yzig pa) 'notes taken down, and extracts made, during a course of study'. - 2. v. the following article.

I. vb., 1. (W. *rág-ċe* (rag che) = *nyúg-ċe* (nyug che), the latter being more in use) to touch, to come in contact with, lág-pa sá-la gar rég-par (lag pa sa la gar reg par) where his hands touched the ground Dzl.; rluṅ yál-ga-la rég-na (rlung yal ga la reg na) when the wind touches the branches Dzl.; ḳá-reg-pa (kha reg pa) c.dat.: to eat, to taste, to take, dúg-la-ḳá-reg ré-ba yod (dug la kha reg re ba yod) in taking poison there is hope, (viz. so bad are the times) Ma.; *tsá-big żal rag dzod* (tsha big zhal rag mdzod) or *żál-la rag* (zhal la rag) W. please, taste a little of it! sá-la ḳru gaṅ tsam-gyis ma rég-par byóṅ-pa (sa la khru gang tsam gyis ma reg par byon pa) to walk not touching the ground by an ell, i.e. to move in the air, about a cubit distant from the ground Pth.; rég-pa-med-pa (reg pa med pa) intangible, unapproachable, out of reach, Glr. - 2. to feel, to perceive Cs.? -- II. sbst. reg (reg) (prob. only abbreviation of reg-bya (reg bya)) feeling, touch, sense of feeling S.g. 10, 5? Comp. reg-dúg (reg dug) ('poison that has entered the body by contact') S.g. 29, is said to signify now in C. venereal disease, syphilis. -- rég-bya (reg bya) 1. what is felt or may be felt, anything palpable or tangible, reg-bya mi tsor (reg bya mi tshor) what may be felt is felt no longer Wdn. 2. feeling, sense of feeling, págs-pa-reg-bya (pags pa reg bya) the feeling of the skin, lús-po pyii rég-bya gráṅ-la (lus po phyi'i reg bya grang la) whilst the outside of the body appears cold to the touch, rég-bya-rtsúb (reg bya rtsub) rough to the touch Med. -- rég-ma (reg ma) Cs. n. of a goddess.
